title: A Psalm of David, upon the eighth.

verses.t: O Lord, rebuke me not in thy wrath, neither chasten me in thine anger.
Have mercy upon me, O Lord; for I am weak: heal me, O Lord; for my bones are vexed.
My soul also is grievously vexed: but thou, O Lord, how long?
Return, O Lord, deliver my soul: save me for thy mercy's sake.
For in death there is no one that remembers thee: and in Hades who will give thee thanks?
I am wearied with my groaning; I shall wash my bed every night; I shall water my couch with my tears.
Mine eye is troubled because of wrath; I am worn out because of all mine enemies.
Depart from me, all ye that work iniquity; for the Lord has heard the voice of my weeping.
The Lord has heard my supplication; the Lord has accepted my prayer.
Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore troubled: let them be turned back and grievously put to shame speedily.
